page: Vultr voc-m-32c-256gb-1600s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this server provides 32 vCPUs and 32 physical cores running at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The CPU features a 32 KB L1 data cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache, with support for AVX, AES, SHA_NI, and BMI2 instruction sets. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core.

For storage, the instance includes a bundled 1600 GB NVMe SSD, offering high-speed local storage capacity. Network connectivity includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates strong processing capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance test scores 3113.46, placing it in the top tier (top 10%). Multi-core performance is also robust, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 99821.78, ranking in the top 25% of tested systems.

From a resource density perspective, the server offers high qualitative cost efficiency by bundling a substantial 256.0 GB memory footprint and 1600 GB of NVMe storage with 32 cores. However, users should note that the CPU allocation is shared, which may introduce performance variability under sustained, highly concurrent workloads compared to dedicated thread allocations. This hardware profile is well-suited for memory-intensive applications, large-scale databases, data warehousing, caching layers, and enterprise web serving.
description: Vultr voc-m-32c-256gb-1600s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring an AMD EPYC Milan processor with 32 vCPUs and 32 physical cores running at 2.0 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a generous 8.0 GB per core, alongside a bundled 1600 GB NVMe SSD for high-speed local storage. In benchmark evaluations, the server delivers top-tier single-core performance with a stress-ng score of 3113.46 (top 10%) and strong multi-core performance scoring 99821.78 (top 25%). While the shared CPU allocation presents a potential tradeoff for sustained peak workloads, the high memory density and fast local storage make this instance highly cost-efficient for memory-intensive applications, database hosting, and enterprise-grade web serving.
og_description: Vultr voc-m-32c-256gb-1600s-amd is an AMD EPYC Milan server with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and a 1600 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier single-core and strong multi-core benchmark performance.
meta_description: Vultr voc-m-32c-256gb-1600s-amd features 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and a 1600 GB NVMe SSD for memory-intensive and database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server combining thirty-two AMD EPYC cores with high-capacity NVMe storage for demanding database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC Milan processor with 32 vCPUs and 32 physical cores
256.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Bundled 1600 GB NVMe SSD for high-speed local storage
Top-tier single-core and strong multi-core stress-ng benchmark results
Shared CPU allocation suitable for memory-heavy and database workloads
